1.11a: Mortgage Processing Pre Completion, Lloyds Bank plc, PO Box 548, Leeds, LS1 1WU
1.11b: Contact Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a
1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: The firm cannot act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
The firm may only act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a separate partner acts for the lender.
1.15: The firm cannot act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
The firm may only act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a separate partner acts for the lender.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is only required if specifically requested.
3.1.4: The seller must have legal representation.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents (List A): Employer's ID card with photo and signature (if employer is known to the firm).

Acceptable identity documents (List B): Employer's ID card with photo and signature (if employer unknown), current UK paper driving licence, Inland Revenue Sub-Contractor's Certificate, Benefits Agency book or notification letter, Inland Revenue Tax Code Notification, EU Residence Permit, Local council rent card or tenancy agreement.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is only required if specifically requested.
3.2.4: The seller must have legal representation.
4.5a: Contact Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a for re-inspection.
4.5b: Submit Certificate of Title via Lender Exchange or post to Halifax Plc, PO Box 548, Leeds, LS1 1WU.
4.1: Physical property inspections are not guaranteed.
Valuation reports are provided in non-forwardable electronic formats.
Conveyancers must obtain a copy of the valuation report directly from the customer.
4.3: Refer to section 4.1 regarding valuation reports.
5.1.1: For remortgages, the customer must have owned the property for at least six months unless inherited (written confirmation of inheritance required).
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are prohibited.
Transactions involving assignable contracts, fulfillment contracts, assisted contracts, or irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited.
Report any Unilateral Notice registered against the freehold title relating to the security address.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts or irrevocable powers of attorney in favor of intervening sellers are not accepted.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm is a member of a trade body with established standards and insurance, and participates in the Property Ombudsman scheme.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted subject to Part 1, Section 9.
5.5.3a: Report evidence of breaches or unsatisfied conditions to Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a.
5.5.3b: Original or copy planning permissions are not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy building regulation consents are not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required.
5.5.4: Report properties subject to the First Homes Scheme if the mortgage offer does not mention it.
Requirements for resale price covenants in Affordable Housing Guidance notes must be met.
Consent for restrictions is deemed granted if scheme requirements are met.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they comprise only part of the title.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds to Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into up to four flats are accepted if the borrower occupies one or owns the freehold and others are on long leases.
Notify the lender if the valuer did not mention the title arrangement or if no physical inspection occurred.
No owner-occupancy requirement applies for Buy to Let (BTL) transactions.
5.8.5: For buildings with two leasehold flats where cross-reversion occurs: Lender requires a mortgage of the borrower's leasehold interest.
In specific cases, a mortgage of both the leasehold interest and the freehold interest in the other flat is required.
5.9.1: Lending on commonhold is not permitted.
5.10.1: Report use restrictions to Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a.
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from immediate family members are accepted without reporting if for owner-occupation and non-repayable.
Immediate family includes: birth/blood relatives, spouse, civil partner, children, step-children, in-laws, and co-habitees.
Gifts from friends or employers are prohibited.
Deeds of Trust or Second Charges to protect gifts are not accepted.
Gifted deposits for BTL where the vendor is a family member are prohibited.
A signed letter from the donor (dated within 3 months) detailing relationship, source of funds, and non-repayability is required.
Bankruptcy searches are required for all parties contributing to the purchase price.
Report non-standard balance of price funding to ConveyancerSupportTeam@lloydsbanking.com.
5.14.15a: Report absentee/insolvent landlords to Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is mandatory if the landlord is absent or insolvent.
5.14.17a: Post-completion documents required: executed Leaseholder Certificate, confirmation of submission to landlord, and any Landlord Certificate.
5.14.17b: Report non-qualifying leaseholders or remediation work costs before completion.
Do not complete until written instructions are received.
5.14.17c: Building safety instructions (5.14.17 a and b) do not apply to remortgages.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 70 years from the date of the mortgage.
5.14.9: Contact Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a for lease matters.
5.14.10: Contact Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Refer unsatisfactory lease terms to Mortgage Processing.
5.14.12: Clear ground rent or service charge receipts are not required by the lender.
5.14.13: Evidence of service of notice is not required by the lender.
5.15.2a: Report management company issues to Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association are not required.
5.17.5a: Send a certified copy of the Power of Attorney after completion if used for execution.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation is not required.
5.19.1: Lending on Shared Equity is restricted to government or social housing schemes from 16 January 2023.
Private company shared equity schemes are not accepted.
5.20.1: Report leases of roof space for solar panels that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.4: Disclosure of Green Deal Plans is not required.
6.1.3: Contact Mortgage Processing as per section 1.11a if the borrower is not proceeding.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to Mortgage Processing.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to Mortgage Processing.
6.4.4: Report cash incentives if they exceed 5% of the purchase price.
Report cash incentives if they are 10% or more greater than stated in the Mortgage Offer.
Report all cash incentives if no details are in the Mortgage Offer.
Report non-financial incentives like cars, but not property fixtures (e.g., kitchens, white goods, carpets).
Email details to ConveyancerSupportTeam@lloydsbanking.com; do not send forms unless requested.
6.5.1: Report absence of vacant possession to Mortgage Processing.
6.6.1: Report letting arrangements to Mortgage Processing.
6.6.3: Copies of tenancy agreements are not required.
6.6.4: Contact Mortgage Processing regarding properties defined as Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO).
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: ABC+, Advantage HCI, Aedis/HomeProof, Ark Insurance, Build Warranty Group, BLP, Buildzone, Cadis, Checkmate, CIR, Compariqo, FMB/Build Assure, Global Home Warranties, ICW, NHBC, One Guarantee, Premier Guarantee, Protek, Q Assure, Thomas Miller.
Warranty required only for first occupancy of new properties.
6.7.2: New home warranty documentation should not be sent.
6.8.1: Retentions for unadopted roads or sewers are not required if no bond/agreement exists, but the borrower must be advised of potential liability.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required for all occupants aged 17 or over, excluding the borrower's children or children of the borrower's co-habitant.
7.4: Report cases where a sole borrower is married, in a civil partnership, or co-habiting; obtain consent to mortgage from the partner.
8.1: Conveyancers may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Send the indemnity insurance policy schedule after completion.
9.2: Indemnity limit must equal the value of the property.
10.2a: Advances are paid electronically via CHAPS with no standard fee.
10.2b: Minimum notice for funds: 3 full working days.
Urgent requests (less than 3 days) incur a £20 CHAPS fee deducted from the advance.
10.3: No standard deductions are made from the advance.
10.7: Delayed funds must be returned electronically within 1 working day.
For roll numbers starting '40/': Sort code 30-15-99, Account 00578907.
Reason codes: RAD (Returned Advance), FADV (Returned further advance), DUP (Duplicate funds).
10.10: Advance may be held for maximum 1 working day.
10.11: Interest is charged at the contractual rate from the date funds were sent if return is delayed.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds and title documents are not required; the firm must retain copies.

14.2.2: Required originals: Consent to mortgage (L26), Shared ownership possession undertaking.

Required copies: Licence to assign, executed Leaseholder Certificate and submission confirmation, Landlord Certificate, notice of mortgage service, management company memorandum/articles, Section 442 Agreement.
For First Homes: Authority to Exchange and Compliance Certificate.
For Lend A Hand: Savings account charge and solicitor's covering letter.
16.1.1: Contact for title documents: Secured Assets, Gloucester. Tel: 0345 8355721. Email: MortgageServicing@LloydsBanking.com.
Provide at least 5 working days notice.

16.3.7b: Standard attestation: 'Executed as a Deed by [] as Attorney for and on behalf of Lloyds Bank plc in the presence of:'.

16.3.1: Standard form L1 for Deed of Covenant is required from the Customer Service Centre.
